title: Code Faster: AI as Your Co-Pilot

modules.title: Meet the AI That Writes Code
modules.sub: Discover what AI coding assistants can and cannot do
modules.description: Learners get hands-on with an AI coding assistant for the first time, exploring how it generates code from natural language prompts. They experiment with different requests and start building intuition for where the tool is genuinely helpful versus where it falls short.

modules.title: Debug It, Don't Just Accept It
modules.sub: Spot, question, and fix errors in AI-generated code
modules.description: Learners receive a set of AI-generated code samples — some subtly broken, some misleading — and must read, test, and correct them. This module builds the critical habit of treating AI output as a first draft that always needs a human eye.

modules.title: Build Something Real Together
modules.sub: Co-create a working mini-project using AI assistance
modules.description: Learners plan and complete a small coding project by strategically deciding which parts to write themselves and which to delegate to the AI assistant. They reflect on how their own understanding deepened — or didn't — depending on how they chose to use the tool.

modules.title: Who Owns This Code, Anyway?
modules.sub: Explore authorship, credit, and responsibility in AI coding
modules.description: Learners investigate real questions around ownership and ethics when AI writes your code — including issues of credit, plagiarism, and accountability when something goes wrong. They form and defend their own positions on where human responsibility begins and ends.

synopsis: You're a developer on a deadline and an AI coding assistant just offered to write your entire program — this story puts you in the driver's seat of decisions about when to use it, when to override it, and how to actually understand what it produces. You'll finish with a working mini-project and a sharp sense of what AI coding tools can and can't replace.
rationale: AI coding assistants are reshaping what it means to learn programming, and students who understand how to collaborate with — rather than blindly rely on — these tools will have a decisive advantage in every technical field.
learning_outcome: Students can use an AI coding assistant to accelerate a real coding task, identify and correct errors in AI-generated code, and explain the difference between using AI as a learning scaffold versus a shortcut.
source_signals: Show HN: I quit coding years ago. AI brought me back
Show HN: PickCode – An educational coding environment for students after Scratch
